# ibek: IOC Builder for EPICS and Kubernetes

{{ code_ci }} {{ docs_ci }} {{ coverage }} {{ pypi_version }} {{ black }} {{ license }}

This tool supports building container images for Generic EPICS IOCs and
generating IOC Instances from those Generic IOCs at container runtime. It is
targetted at running IOCs in Kubernetes, but the images it
creates can execute in any container runtime, such as docker or podman.

> ```
>
>
>
>
> PyPI
>
> pip install ibek
>
> Source code
>
> https://github.com/epics-containers/ibek
>
> Documentation
>
> https://epics-containers.github.io/ibek
>
> Releases
>
> https://github.com/epics-containers/ibek/releases
> ```

# UPDATE: Version 2.0.0

There have been a number of breaking changes since version 1.8.0. These are
primarily to support RTEMS cross compiled IOCs. But we have also taken the
opportunity to clarify the CLI.

All changes are compatible with 2.0.0 of ibek-support and 3.4.0 of all other
epics-containers repos. When using ioc-template 3.4.0 to update your generic
IOCs, make sure you also update the ibek-support submodule to 2.0.0 or newer,
then you should have all compatible versions.

______________________________________________________________________

The documentation is still under construction. For the moment the best
pages to read are the following:

- [Modules, Definitions and Entities](https://epics-containers.github.io/ibek/main/developer/explanations/entities.html//)
  A description of the basic concepts of ibek.
- [naming conventions in support YAML](https://epics-containers.github.io/ibek/main/user/reference/naming.html)
  Conventions for the YAML that ibek consumes.
- [epics-containers documentation](https://epics-containers.github.io)
  Documentation and tutorials for the epics-containers suite of tools.

The command line interface for ibek is as follows. The most up to date version
of this can be found by running `ibek --help`.

```
[container][~]$ ibek --help
Usage: ibek [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

  IOC Builder for EPICS and Kubernetes

  Provides support for building generic EPICS IOC container images and for
  running IOC instances in a Kubernetes cluster.

Options:
  --version                       Print the version of ibek and exit
  --install-completion [bash|zsh|fish|powershell|pwsh]
                                  Install completion for the specified shell.
  --show-completion [bash|zsh|fish|powershell|pwsh]
                                  Show completion for the specified shell, to
                                  copy it or customize the installation.
  --help                          Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  support  Commands for building support modules during container build
  ioc      Commands for building generic IOCs during container build
  runtime  Commands for building IOC instance startup files at container...
[container][~]$ ibek support --help
Usage: ibek support [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

  Commands for building support modules during container build

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  apt-install         Install debian packages into the container.
  git-clone           clone a support module from a remote repository
  register            prepare the configure RELEASE files to build a...
  add-libs            declare the libraries for this support module for...
  add-dbds            declare the dbd files for this support module for...
  add-release-macro   add or replace a macro the global RELEASE file
  add-config-macro    add or replace a macro in...
  add-to-config-site  add some text to a support module's CONFIG_SITE file
  compile             compile a support module after preparation with...
  generate-links      generate symlinks to the bob, pvi and support YAML...
  generate-schema     Produce JSON global schema for all...
[container][~]$ ibek ioc --help
Usage: ibek ioc [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

  Commands for building generic IOCs during container build

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  build-docker            EXPERIMENTAL: Attempt to interpret the...
  generate-schema         Create a json schema from a number of...
  extract-runtime-assets  Find all the runtime assets in an EPICS...
  make-source-template    Create a new IOC boilerplate source tree in the...
  generate-makefile       get the dbd and lib files from all support...
  compile                 Compile a generic IOC after support modules are...
  build                   A convenience function that calls...
[container][~]$ ibek runtime --help
Usage: ibek runtime [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

  Commands for building IOC instance startup files at container runtime

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  generate  Build a startup script for an IOC instance
```

% Anything below this line is used when viewing README.rst and will be replaced
% when included in index.rst

See <https://epics-containers.github.io/ibek> for current documentation.

functions.name: ibek.commands.semver_compare
documentation: Compare two semVer strings similarly to pip's requirements specifier syntax

modules.name: ibek.ioc_factory
documentation: functions for making Entity models

classes.name: ibek.ioc_factory.IocFactory

methods.name: ibek.ioc_factory.IocFactory.deserialize_ioc
documentation:
Takes an ioc instance entities file, list of generic ioc yaml files.

Returns a model of the resulting ioc instance

methods.name: ibek.ioc_factory.IocFactory.make_ioc_model
documentation:
Create an IOC derived model, by setting its entities attribute to
be of type 'list of Entity derived classes'.

Also instantiate any SubEntities at this time
A Class for creating Generic IOC classes and instances

modules.name: ibek.gen_scripts
documentation: Functions for building the db and boot scripts

functions.name: ibek.gen_scripts.create_db_script
documentation: Create make_db.sh script for expanding the database templates

functions.name: ibek.gen_scripts.create_boot_script
documentation: Create the boot script for an IOC

modules.name: ibek.utils
documentation:
A class containing utility functions for passing into the Jinja context.

This allows us to provide simple functions that can be called inside
Jinja templates with {{ _global.function_name() }}. It also allows
us to maintain state between calls to the Jinja templates because
we pass a single instance of this class into all Jinja contexts.

classes.name: ibek.utils.Utils

methods.name: ibek.utils.Utils.__reset__
documentation:
Reset all saved state. For use in testing where more than one
IOC is rendered in a single session

methods.name: ibek.utils.Utils.set_file_name
documentation: Set the ioc name based on the file name of the instance definition

methods.name: ibek.utils.Utils.set_ioc_name
documentation: Set the ioc name based on the file name of the instance definition

methods.name: ibek.utils.Utils.get_env
documentation: Get an environment variable

methods.name: ibek.utils.Utils.set
documentation: create a global variable for our jinja context

methods.name: ibek.utils.Utils.get
documentation: get the value a global variable for our jinja context

methods.name: ibek.utils.Utils.incrementor
documentation:
get a named counter that increments by inc each time it is called

creates a new counter if it does not yet exist

methods.name: ibek.utils.Utils.render
documentation: Render a Jinja template with the global _global object in the context

methods.name: ibek.utils.Utils.render_map
documentation:
Render a map of jinja templates with values from the given context.

If given a key with a value of `None`, the key itself will be used as a template
value, so `{"P": None}` is equivalent to `{"P": "{{ P }}"}`.

Args:

: context: Context to extract template variables from
  map: Map of macro to jinja template to render
A Utility class for adding functions to the Jinja context

modules.name: ibek.parameters
documentation: Classes to specify arguments to Entity Models

classes.name: ibek.parameters.DefineType
documentation: The type of a value

classes.name: ibek.parameters.Define
documentation: A calculated value for an Entity Model

classes.name: ibek.parameters.Param
documentation: Base class for all Argument Types

classes.name: ibek.parameters.FloatParam
documentation: An argument with a float value

classes.name: ibek.parameters.StrParam
documentation: An argument with a str value

classes.name: ibek.parameters.IntParam
documentation: An argument with an int value

classes.name: ibek.parameters.BoolParam
documentation: An argument with an bool value

classes.name: ibek.parameters.ObjectParam
documentation: A reference to another entity defined in this IOC

classes.name: ibek.parameters.IdParam
documentation: Explicit ID argument that an object can refer to

classes.name: ibek.parameters.EnumParam
documentation: An argument with an enum value

modules.name: ibek.ioc
documentation:
Classes for generating an IocInstance derived class from a set of
support module YAML files

functions.name: ibek.ioc.clear_entity_model_ids
documentation: Resets the global id_to_entity dict

classes.name: ibek.ioc.EnumVal
documentation: An enum that is printed as its name only

classes.name: ibek.ioc.Entity

methods.name: ibek.ioc.Entity._process_field
documentation:
Process an Entity field - doing jinja rendering, type coercion and
object id storage/lookup as required.

methods.name: ibek.ioc.Entity.add_ibek_attributes
documentation:
Whole Entity model validation

Do jinja rendering of pre_defines/ parameters / post_defines
in the correct order.

Also adds pre_defines and post_defines to the model instance, making
them available for the phase 2 (final) jinja rendering performed in
ibek.runtime_cmds.generate().

methods.name: ibek.ioc.Entity.__str__
documentation:
When a jinja template refers to an object by itself e.g.
    # this is the startup entry for {{ my_entity }}
Jinja will attempt to render the object as a string and this
method will be called.

The behaviour is to print the ID of the object. Thus we look up
which of our object's fields is the ID field and return the
value of that field.
A baseclass for all generated Entity classes.

classes.name: ibek.ioc.IOC
documentation: Used to load an IOC instance entities yaml file into a Pydantic Model.

modules.name: ibek.support
documentation: Support Class to represent a deserialized <MODULE_NAME>.ibek.support.yaml file.

classes.name: ibek.support.Support
documentation: Lists the EntityModels for a support module, this defines what Entities it supports

modules.name: ibek.support_cmds.files
documentation: Functions for inserting snippets into files in an idempotent fashion

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.files.get_config_site_file
documentation:
Return the path to an CONFIG_SITE file for a support module.
Default is /epics/module/configure/CONFIG_SITE.local.linux-x86_64.Common

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.files.get_release_file
documentation:
Return the path to an EPICS RELEASE file for a support
default is /epics/module/configure/RELEASE.local

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.files.add_list_to_file
documentation:
add a sequence of strings into a file, leaving previously existing
strings where they are

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.files.add_text_once
documentation:
Idempotent add of text to a file.
Creates the file if it doesn't exist.

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.files.symlink_files
documentation:
Symlink files matching the given pattern in source directory to target directory.

Args:
    source_directory: Directory containing source files
    file_pattern: Pattern of files in source directory to be symlinked
    target_directory: Directory to create symlinks in

modules.name: ibek.support_cmds.checks
documentation: Helper functions for the ibek support commands.

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.checks.verify_release_includes_local
documentation:
Make sure that a module uses RELEASE.local.

A git-dirtying patch is required if not.

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.checks.do_dependencies
documentation: Fix up the global release file to include all support modules registered

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.checks.check_deps
documentation: Check if specified dependencies have been supplied

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.checks.add_macro
documentation: add or replace a macro in a RELEASE or CONFIG file

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.checks.validate_support
documentation:
Validate that the support folder exists and setup initial template files
if required

modules.name: ibek.entity_model
documentation: The EntityModel class describes what a given support module can instantiate.

functions.name: ibek.entity_model.default
documentation: defines a default type which may be

classes.name: ibek.entity_model.Database
documentation: A database file that should be loaded by the startup script and its args

classes.name: ibek.entity_model.EnvironmentVariable
documentation: An environment variable that should be set in the startup script

classes.name: ibek.entity_model.Comment
documentation:
A script snippet that will have '# ' prepended to every line
for insertion into the startup script

classes.name: ibek.entity_model.Text
documentation: A script snippet to insert into the startup script

classes.name: ibek.entity_model.EntityPVI
documentation: Entity PVI definition

classes.name: ibek.entity_model.EntityModel

methods.name: ibek.entity_model.EntityModel._get_id_arg
documentation:
Lookup which of this object's fields represents its ID. The ID is the
field of type IdParam whose value is the identifying name for
this object.

The jinja templates of other objects can refer to this object by the
value of its ID field.
A Model for a class of Entity that an IOC instance may instantiate

functions.name: ibek.ioc_cmds.commands.build_docker

params.name:
typer.Option(
            help="The filepath to the Dockerfile to build",
            autocompletion=lambda: [],  # Forces path autocompletion
        )

documentation:
EXPERIMENTAL: Attempt to interpret the Dockerfile and run it's commands
inside the devcontainer. For internal, incremental builds of the Dockerfile.

Useful for debugging the Dockerfile without having to build the whole
container from outside of the IOC devcontainer.

functions.name: ibek.ioc_cmds.commands.generate_schema

params.name:
typer.Option(
            help="The file path to the schema file to be written",
            autocompletion=lambda: [],  # Forces path autocompletion
        )

documentation:
Create a json schema from a number of support_module.ibek.support.yaml
files

functions.name: ibek.ioc_cmds.commands.extract_runtime_assets
documentation:
Find all the runtime assets in an EPICS installation and copy them to a
new folder hierarchy for packaging into a container runtime stage.

This should be performed in a throw away container stage (runtime_prep)
as it is destructive of the source folder, because it uses move for speed.

functions.name: ibek.ioc_cmds.assets.move_file
documentation:
Move a file / tree / symlink from src to dest, stripping symbols from
binaries if they are in the binary list.

functions.name: ibek.ioc_cmds.assets.extract_assets
documentation: extract and copy runtime assets from a completed developer stage container

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands._install_debs
documentation:
Install a list of debian packages.

If they have an http:// or https://
prefix then they will be downloaded and installed from file.

args: debs: List[str] - list of debian packages to install - can also include
                        any additional 'apt-get install' options required

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands.apt_install
documentation: Install packages

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands.add_runtime_packages
documentation:
Add packages to RUNTIME_DEBS for later install with apt_install_runtime_packages

The list may include any additional 'apt-get install' options required.

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands.apt_install_runtime_packages
documentation: Install packages from the list collected by calls to add_runtime_packages

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands.git_clone
documentation:
clone a support module from a remote repository

Add any additional arguments to the git clone command at the end of the
argument list.

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands.register

params.name:
typer.Option(
            help="path to support module",
            autocompletion=lambda: [],  # Forces path autocompletion
        )

documentation:
prepare the configure RELEASE files to build a support module
inside an epics-containers build

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands.add_libs
documentation: declare the libraries for this support module for inclusion in IOC Makefile

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands.add_dbds
documentation: declare the dbd files for this support module for inclusion in IOC Makefile

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands.add_release_macro
documentation: add or replace a macro the global RELEASE file

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands.add_config_macro
documentation: add or replace a macro in CONFIG_SITE.linux-x86_64.Common file

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands.add_to_config_site
documentation: add some text to a support module's CONFIG_SITE file

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands.check_dependencies
documentation: Check if specified dependencies have been supplied

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands.compile
documentation:
compile a support module after preparation with `ibek support register` etc.

Add any extra compiler options to the end of the argument list

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands.generate_links

params.name:
typer.Argument(
            help="ibek-support(-xxx) folder to generate links for",
            autocompletion=lambda: [],  # Forces path autocompletion
        )

documentation:
Generate symlinks to the ibek and pvi YAML files for a compiled IOC.

Args:
    folder: path to an ibek-support folder containing the YAML files
            for the support module to link to. This should be a sub
            module of the ioc-xxx Generic IOC project and may be the
            public ibek-support repo or a private ibek-support-xxx repo.

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands.generate_schema

params.name:
typer.Option(
            help="The filename to write the schema to",
            autocompletion=lambda: [],  # Forces path autocompletion
        )

documentation: Produce JSON global schema for all <support_module>.ibek.support.yaml files

functions.name: ibek.support_cmds.commands.add_runtime_files
documentation:
Adds to the list of folders or filesthat are copied over to the runtime
stage of the container build.

modules.name: ibek._version
documentation: file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

functions.name: ibek.runtime_cmds.commands.generate
documentation: Build a startup script for an IOC instance

functions.name: ibek.runtime_cmds.commands.generate_pvi
documentation:
Generate pvi bob and template files to add to UI index and IOC database.

Args:
    ioc: IOC instance to extract entity pvi definitions from

Returns:
    List of bob files to add as buttons on index and databases to add to IOC
    substitution file

functions.name: ibek.runtime_cmds.commands.generate_index
documentation:
Generate an index bob using pvi.

Args:
    title: Title of index UI
    index_entries: List of entries to include as buttons on index UI

functions.name: ibek.dev_cmds.commands.instance
documentation:
Symlink an IOC instance config folder into /epics/ioc/config.

Used in the devcontainer to allow the IOC instance to be run using
/epics/ioc/starts.sh. Changes made to the config will be immediately
available and also under version control.

e.g. if instance is /workspaces/bl38p/iocs/bl38p-mo-panda-01 then we need:
- /epics/ioc/config -> /workspaces/bl38p/iocs/bl38p-mo-panda-01/config

modules.name: ibek.render_db
documentation:
A class for rendering a substitution file from multiple instantiations of
support module yaml files.

methods.name: ibek.render_db.RenderDb.add_row
documentation:
Accumulate rows of arguments for each template file,
Adding a new template file if it does not already exist.
Convert all arguments to strings.

methods.name: ibek.render_db.RenderDb.parse_instances
documentation:
Gather the database template instantiations from all entities
while validating the arguments

methods.name: ibek.render_db.RenderDb.add_database
documentation:
Validate database and add row using entity as context.

Args:
    database: Database to add row for
    entity: Entity to use as context for Jinja template expansion

methods.name: ibek.render_db.RenderDb.add_extra_databases
documentation:
Add databases that are not part of EntityModels

Args:
    databases: Databases to add, each mapped against an Entity to use as context

methods.name: ibek.render_db.RenderDb.align_columns
documentation:
Make sure columns will line up for each template file, also
provide escaping for spaces and quotes

methods.name: ibek.render_db.RenderDb.render_database
documentation:
Render a database substitution file.

Args:
    extra_databases: Databases to add that are not included on an Entity

modules.name: ibek.render
documentation: Functions for rendering lines in the boot script using Jinja2

classes.name: ibek.render.Render

methods.name: ibek.render.Render.render_text
documentation:
Add in the next line of text, honouring the once flag which will
only add the line once per IOC.

Jinja rendering of values/args has already been done in Entity.__post_init__
but we pass all strings though jinja again to render any other jinja
in the IOC (e.g. database and function entries)

once uses the name of the model + suffix to track which lines
have been rendered already. The suffix can be used where a given
Entity has more than one element to render once (e.g. functions)

methods.name: ibek.render.Render.render_pre_ioc_init
documentation:
render the startup script by combining the jinja template from
an entity with the arguments from an Entity

methods.name: ibek.render.Render.render_post_ioc_init
documentation:
render the post-iocInit entries by combining the jinja template
from an entity with the arguments from an Entity

methods.name: ibek.render.Render.render_environment_variables
documentation:
render the environment variable elements by combining the jinja template
from an entity with the arguments from an Entity

methods.name: ibek.render.Render.render_elements
documentation: Render elements of a given IOC instance based on calling the correct method

methods.name: ibek.render.Render.render_pre_ioc_init_elements
documentation: Render all of the startup script entries for a given IOC instance

methods.name: ibek.render.Render.render_post_ioc_init_elements
documentation: Render all of the post-iocInit elements for a given IOC instance

methods.name: ibek.render.Render.render_environment_variable_elements
documentation: Render all of the environment variable entries for a given IOC instance

methods.name: ibek.entity_factory.EntityFactory.__init__

A class to create `Entity` models from `EntityModel`s.

Created models are stored in `self._entity_models` to lookup when
resolving nested `SubEntity`s.

documentation:
Read a set of *.ibek.support.yaml files and generate Entity classes
from their EntityModel entries

methods.name: ibek.entity_factory.EntityFactory._make_entity_model
documentation: Create an Entity Model from a EntityModel instance and a Support instance.

methods.name: ibek.entity_factory.EntityFactory._make_entity_models
documentation:
Create Entity subclasses for all EntityModel instances in the given
Support instance. Returns a list of the Entity subclasses Models.

methods.name: ibek.entity_factory.EntityFactory.resolve_sub_entities
documentation: Recursively resolve SubEntity collections in a list of Entity instances
